questions.question: The Panchayats Extension to Scheduled Areas (PESA) Act, 1996 applies to the Scheduled Areas of which two states in India?
questions.explanation: PESA Act 1996 is applicable to West Bengal and Odisha.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, has provision for the reservation of seats for SC and ST in Panchayats in which ratio?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act has provision for the reservation of seats for SC and ST in Panchayats in 1/3 and 1/4 ratio.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct article of the Indian Constitution that emphasizes the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Article 38 of the Indian Constitution emphasizes the Directive Principles of State Policy.

questions.question: The concept of 'Eka Pramukh' in the context of the Panchayat system is related to?
questions.explanation: Eka Pramukh refers to the single-headed structure of the Gram Panchayat.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct name of the state election commission in West Bengal?
options: State Election Commission of West Bengal
Commissionerate of State Election of West Bengal
questions.explanation: The correct name of the state election commission in West Bengal is the State Election Commission of West Bengal.

questions.question: Which river originates from the Himalayas and flows through the Darjeeling district in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Tista River is a significant river in West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following hills is a part of the Western Plateau and is found in the Bankura district of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Eastern Ghat is a mountain range in eastern India.

questions.question: Which of the following is a deltaic plain in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Sunderbans Delta is the largest deltaic plain in West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain passes is located in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Jelep La Pass is a mountain pass in the Darjeeling district.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers originates from the Jogeshwar Hills in the Purulia district of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Barakar River originates from the Jogeshwar Hills in Purulia.

questions.question: The West Bengal government launched a scheme to provide financial assistance to the families of martyrs. What is the name of this scheme?
options: Swami Vivekananda Merit Cum Means Scholarship
Karmachari Kalyan Board's Financial Assistance Scheme
Karmachari Kalyan Board's Widow Financial Assistance Scheme
questions.explanation: Karmachari Kalyan Board's Widow Financial Assistance Scheme provides financial assistance to the families of police martyrs.

questions.question: The Mamata Banerjee government introduced a scheme to provide free cycles to school-going girls. What is the name of this scheme?
questions.explanation: Siksha Sashthi Karmdhane scheme provides free cycles to school-going girls.

questions.question: The West Bengal government launched a scheme to provide financial assistance to the people affected by the Super Cyclone Amphan.
questions.explanation: The government launched the Amphan Relief Package to provide financial assistance to the people affected by the Super Cyclone Amphan.

questions.question: The West Bengal government introduced a scheme to provide healthcare services to the below-poverty-line families.
questions.explanation: Swasthya Sathi scheme provides healthcare services to the below-poverty-line families.

questions.question: The Mamata Banerjee government introduced a scheme to provide assistance to the people affected by the COVID-19 pandemic.
questions.explanation: The government introduced the COVID 19 Assistance Package to provide assistance to the people affected by the COVID-19 pandemic.

questions.question: A person deposits ₹ 50,000 in a fixed deposit account for 2 years at an interest rate of 8% per annum compounded annually. What is the amount of interest earned?
questions.explanation: Interest is calculated using the compound interest formula.

questions.question: What is the simple interest on ₹ 12,000 at 4% per annum for 3 years?
questions.explanation: Simple interest is calculated using the formula: Principal × Rate × Time.

questions.question: A bank offers a compound interest rate of 10% per annum. If ₹ 20,000 is invested for 4 years, what is the total amount after 4 years?
questions.explanation: Compound interest is calculated using the formula: Principal × (1 + Rate)^Time.

questions.question: What is the rate of interest for a deposit of ₹ 60,000 that grows to ₹ 67,800 in 2 years?
questions.explanation: Interest earned is calculated by subtracting the principal from the amount.

questions.question: A person lends ₹ 15,000 at 6% per annum simple interest for 2 years. How much will the person get back after 2 years?
questions.explanation: Simple interest is calculated using the formula: Principal × Rate × Time.

questions.question: A person can finish a work in 90 hours. How many hours will it take to finish the work if 6 people work together?
questions.explanation: Work decreases with the increase of the number of workers.

questions.question: A public water supply system can fill a tank in 8 hours. If the system is closed for 2 hours, how much time will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Time to fill the tank remains the same.

questions.question: A pipe can fill a tank in 8 hours. An outlet pipe can empty the tank in 12 hours. If both pipes work together, how long will it take to empty the tank?
questions.explanation: Net effect will be emptying the tank.

questions.question: A man can do a piece of work in 5 days, while a boy can do the same work in 10 days. If they work together, how many days will they take to complete the work?
questions.explanation: Work decreases with the increase of the number of workers.

questions.question: A water tank can be filled by a pipe in 10 hours. Due to a leakage, it will take 15 hours to fill the tank. How long will it take to fill the tank if the leakage is plugged?
questions.explanation: Time to fill the tank decreases with the decrease of leakage.

questions.question: A series of alphabets are given in a coded form: GURUH, BANGLA, PUNJABI. Which of the following is the correct decoding of the series?
questions.explanation: Each word is the name of a language.

questions.question: If 3x + 2 = 17, then what is the value of 4x - 1?
questions.explanation: Solving the given equation for x.

questions.question: If the code: E means +2, F means -2, then what is the value of G + H?
questions.explanation: Using the given codes.

questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 1, 5, 9, 13, 17, ?
questions.explanation: Identifying the pattern in the series.

questions.question: If the code: A means +3, B means -4, then what is the value of A + B?
questions.explanation: Using the given codes.

questions.question: Aman's mother is the daughter of Ramesh. Ramesh's son is the father of
questions.explanation: Ramesh's son is Aman's father.

questions.question: A woman is the wife of Mr. Roy. She is the only child of her father. What is the relationship between the woman and Mrs. Das?
questions.explanation: The information given does not establish any relationship between the woman and Mrs. Das.

questions.question: In a family, there is a man, his wife, their children, and their grandchildren. If A is the grandson of the man, then which of the following is true?
options: A is the son of the man
A is the son of the man's son
A is the son of the man's daughter
questions.explanation: A is the son of the man.

questions.question: A woman has a child. Her sister also has a child. What is the relationship between the woman and the child of her sister?
questions.explanation: The woman is the aunt of the child of her sister.

questions.question: A and B are two brothers. If C is the father of B, then what is the relationship between A and C?
questions.explanation: A is the brother of B, and therefore the son of C.

questions.question: If 1 is added to the first term, 2 is added to the second term, 3 is added to the third term, and so on, what will be the sum of the series 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, 17, 20, 23, 26, 29?
questions.explanation: The given series is an arithmetic series with a common difference of 3.

questions.question: If the sequence goes like 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, what will be the next term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is formed by adding consecutive multiples of 4 to the previous term.

questions.question: What comes next in the sequence of letters A, D, G, J, M?
questions.explanation: The sequence is formed by adding 3 to the position of the alphabet in the English alphabet.

questions.question: If the sequence is 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, what will be the next term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence consists of squares of consecutive numbers.

questions.question: What comes next in the sequence of letters A, E, I, M, R?
questions.explanation: The sequence is formed by adding 3 to the position of the alphabet in the English alphabet.
